rulings.Case Title: MARY GLASPIE FRANK VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2015 line 1. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A., RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT continued to March 27, 2015. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C and provide courtesy copy of the operative complaint to department 501 no later than March 13, 2015 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. Opposing party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copy of opposition to department 501 no later than March 13, 2015 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GALINDO et al VS. SECURITY TITLE INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Allow Postings In The Civic Center Hotel Per Local Ordinance And For Copies Of Notices
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2015 line 5. PLAINTIFFS PAUL GALINDO, BRNDON JEREZ, JEFFREY MORRIS, LORI MORRIS, CRAIG WENNIHAN, CHRIS MILLS, APRIL CAMPBELL, PAULA BUCKLEY, JOHN ADAMS, JENNIFER HOOPER, BARRY WILDER, JOSEPH CATONE, DAVID GEORGE, CLARENCE PERKINS, MARIA OLGUIN, BELINDA CAMPOS, JOHN STEWART, KOZERA MCFADDEN's Motion To Allow Postings In The Civic Center Hotel Per Local Ordinance And For Copies Of Notices Plaintiffs move for a "preliminary injunction" to allow "postings" on the Defendants' property. The Motion is DENIED for two reasons: (1) the papers do not make the showings required for a preliminary injunction; (2) the "postings" as issue are expressly solicitations for employment by Plaintiffs' attorneys, for which Plaintiffs cite no authority. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: YOUNG SONG VS. AF EVANS PROPERTY MANAGEMENT (EPMI, A BAYSIDE ET AL

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD STOLL VS. PAUL ROBERTSON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2015 line 7. DEFENDANT PAUL ROBERTSON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. The plaintiff alleges that he is the successor in interest to both the property and landlord. This is sufficient. With respect to the defendant's third claim-the attached lease agreement is incomplete-the plaintiff's attachment includes all of the essential terms that are relevant to the complaint as alleged. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC VS. HUGO NELSON CHOX GUARCHAJ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Strike And Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2015 line 8. DEFENDANTS LIEDA VEGA, VILMA VEGA, HUGO NELSON and HUGO PEREZ's Motion To Strike And Tax Costs This case presents a situation no "specified" in CCP 1032 (a)(4). The Court finds no "prevailing party" and awards no costs to either side. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax And Strike Costs

rulings.Case Title: MARIA SAM VS. NOE GUILLEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stiupulation
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2015 line 9. PLAINTIFF MARIA SAM's Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Dismissal And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stiupulation DENIED. When a party alleges breach of an agreement, she is obligated to prove that she did all the significant things the agreement required her to do. Plaintiff has failed to carry hurry burden here. = (501/RBU)
